Longform Test Episode

15 min of audio · ~1,500 words · about 8 min to read · Auto-transcribed with test
Jump to 00:00:00Start 00:03:00This program is brought to you by a sponsor you can safely… 00:06:00This program is brought to you by a sponsor you can safely… 00:09:00This program is brought to you by a sponsor you can safely… 00:12:00This program is brought to you by a sponsor you can safely…

00:00:00 This program is brought to you by a sponsor you can safely skip past. You have to trust that the dots will somehow connect in your future, because believing it gives you confidence. The second story is about love and loss, which changed everything for me. I dropped out of Reed College after the first six months but stayed around another eighteen. My third story is about death, and how remembering it clarifies every big choice.

00:00:45 Stay hungry and stay foolish, and never settle for living someone else's dogma. Calligraphy classes taught me about serif and sans serif typefaces ten years before the Mac. Getting fired from Apple was the best thing that could have happened to me. Remembering that you are going to die is the best way to avoid the trap of thinking you have something to lose. No one wants to die, and yet death is the destination we all share.
